compare.metaTitle: {cityA} vs {cityB} for Digital Nomads | Nomad Guide
compare.metaDesc: Compare {cityA} and {cityB} side by side — cost of living, internet speed, safety, weather, and quality of life data for digital nomads.
compare.dataAttribution: Data from Open-Meteo (weather), Numbeo (quality indices), and global internet speed rankings. Updated monthly.

intro.sameCountry: {cityA} and {cityB} are both in {countryA}, making this one of the most common comparisons for digital nomads planning a base in the region. This guide breaks down the key differences across cost, internet, safety, weather, and quality of life.
intro.sameRegion: {cityA} ({countryA}) and {cityB} ({countryB}) are both in {regionA}, offering different takes on the nomad lifestyle. Here is how they compare across the metrics that matter most.
intro.diffRegion: {cityA} ({countryA}, {regionA}) and {cityB} ({countryB}, {regionB}) represent very different environments for digital nomads. This comparison covers cost, connectivity, safety, weather, and overall quality of life.

cost.aWinsBig_v1: {cityA} is significantly cheaper for digital nomads at ${costA}/mo — {pct}% less than {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo. Over 12 months, the difference adds up to ${annualSavings}.
cost.aWinsBig_v2: Budget-conscious nomads take note: {cityA} costs ${costA}/mo, saving {pct}% compared to {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo — that's ${annualSavings} per year.
cost.aWinsBig_v3: The monthly savings are substantial — {cityA} at ${costA}/mo costs {pct}% less than {cityB} at ${costB}/mo, adding up to ${annualSavings} over a year.
cost.aWinsSmall_v1: {cityA} has a modest cost advantage at ${costA}/mo versus {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo, a {pct}% difference that adds up over a longer stay.
cost.aWinsSmall_v2: The cost difference is noticeable: {cityA} at ${costA}/mo is {pct}% cheaper than {cityB} at ${costB}/mo, worth considering for extended stays.
cost.aWinsSmall_v3: {cityA} edges ahead on affordability at ${costA}/mo compared to {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo — a {pct}% gap that matters over time.
cost.tied: Both cities sit in a similar price range — {cityA} at ${costA}/mo and {cityB} at ${costB}/mo — so cost alone is not a deciding factor here.
cost.bWinsSmall_v1: {cityB} edges ahead on affordability at ${costB}/mo versus {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo, a {pct}% difference worth considering for budget-focused nomads.
cost.bWinsSmall_v2: The cost difference is noticeable: {cityB} at ${costB}/mo is {pct}% cheaper than {cityA} at ${costA}/mo, worth considering for extended stays.
cost.bWinsSmall_v3: {cityB} has a modest cost advantage at ${costB}/mo compared to {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo — a {pct}% gap that matters over time.
cost.bWinsBig_v1: {cityB} is the clear budget winner at ${costB}/mo — {pct}% cheaper than {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo, a saving of ${annualSavings} per year.
cost.bWinsBig_v2: Budget-conscious nomads take note: {cityB} costs ${costB}/mo, saving {pct}% compared to {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo — that's ${annualSavings} per year.
cost.bWinsBig_v3: The monthly savings are substantial — {cityB} at ${costB}/mo costs {pct}% less than {cityA} at ${costA}/mo, adding up to ${annualSavings} over a year.

internet.aWinsBig_v1: {cityA} leads on internet connectivity at {speedA} Mbps (ranked #{rankA} globally) versus {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}). The speed gap is notable for video-heavy remote work.
internet.aWinsBig_v2: Remote workers will appreciate {cityA}'s {speedA} Mbps (global #{rankA}) — considerably faster than {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}).
internet.aWinsBig_v3: For bandwidth-intensive work, {cityA} has a clear edge at {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA} globally) compared to {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}).
internet.aWinsSmall_v1: {cityA} has a slight lead on internet at {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA} globally) compared to {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}). Both support standard remote work.
internet.aWinsSmall_v2: Internet speeds are comparable: {cityA} at {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA}) slightly outpaces {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}).
internet.aWinsSmall_v3: {cityA} edges ahead with {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA} globally) versus {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}) — a modest difference for most workflows.
internet.tied: Internet speeds are nearly identical — {cityA} at {speedA} Mbps and {cityB} at {speedB} Mbps. Both handle video calls and uploads well.
internet.bWinsSmall_v1: {cityB} has a slight lead on internet at {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB} globally) compared to {cityA}'s {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA}). Both support standard remote work.
internet.bWinsSmall_v2: Internet speeds are comparable: {cityB} at {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB}) slightly outpaces {cityA}'s {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA}).
internet.bWinsSmall_v3: {cityB} edges ahead with {speedB} Mbps (#{rankB} globally) versus {cityA}'s {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA}) — a modest difference for most workflows.
internet.bWinsBig_v1: {cityB} leads on internet connectivity at {speedB} Mbps (ranked #{rankB} globally) versus {cityA}'s {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA}). The speed gap is notable for video-heavy remote work.
internet.bWinsBig_v2: Remote workers will appreciate {cityB}'s {speedB} Mbps (global #{rankB}) — considerably faster than {cityA}'s {speedA} Mbps (#{rankA}).

verdict.aWins_v1: Overall, {cityA} edges ahead for most digital nomads — at ${costA}/mo it runs {pct}% cheaper than {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo, with {speedA} Mbps internet for reliable remote work.
verdict.aWins_v2: For the typical digital nomad, {cityA} is the stronger pick. At ${costA}/mo it beats {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo by {pct}%, and the {speedA} Mbps internet handles video calls without issues.
verdict.aWins_v3: The data favors {cityA} as the more well-rounded choice. At ${costA}/mo versus {cityB}'s ${costB}/mo, that is a {pct}% saving that adds up significantly on an extended stay.
verdict.bWins_v1: Overall, {cityB} edges ahead for most digital nomads — at ${costB}/mo it runs {pct}% cheaper than {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo, with {speedB} Mbps internet for reliable remote work.
verdict.bWins_v2: For the typical digital nomad, {cityB} is the stronger pick. At ${costB}/mo it beats {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo by {pct}%, and the {speedB} Mbps internet handles video calls without issues.
verdict.bWins_v3: The data favors {cityB} as the more well-rounded choice. At ${costB}/mo versus {cityA}'s ${costA}/mo, that is a {pct}% saving that adds up significantly on an extended stay.
verdict.tied_v1: This one is genuinely close. Both {cityA} (${costA}/mo) and {cityB} (${costB}/mo) offer strong value for digital nomads — the right choice depends on your personal priorities.
verdict.tied_v2: There is no clear overall winner — {cityA} at ${costA}/mo and {cityB} at ${costB}/mo are in the same price bracket. Your lifestyle priorities will be the tiebreaker.
verdict.tied_v3: {cityA} (${costA}/mo) and {cityB} (${costB}/mo) are well-matched overall. Budget, weather preferences, and lifestyle priorities will determine the better fit.
